mission.title: Install and Configure Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS)
mission.description: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the process of running Kubernetes on AWS. It provides a highly available and secure Kubernetes environment, but users should be aware of potential costs associated with AWS resources. EKS is ideal for organizations looking to leverage AWS infrastructure while maintaining Kubernetes flexibility.

steps.title: Install the AWS CLI
steps.description:
The AWS CLI is required to interact with AWS services, including EKS.

```bash

pip install awscli --upgrade --user

aws --version

```

> Install the AWS CLI using pip.
> Verify the installation.

steps.title: Create an EKS Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates an EKS cluster with the specified Kubernetes version and node configuration.

```bash

aws eks create-cluster --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --role-arn arn:aws:iam::YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID:role/EKS-Cluster-Role --resources-vpc-config subnetIds=subnet-12345678,subnet-87654321

```

> Replace YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID with your actual AWS account ID.

steps.title: Configure kubectl
steps.description:
This step configures kubectl to use the newly created EKS cluster.

```bash

aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks if the nodes in the EKS cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up monitoring and autoscaling for your EKS cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/aws/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/metrics-server/master/deploy/1.8+/metrics-server-deployment.yaml

```

> This command installs the cluster autoscaler.
> This command installs the metrics server.

uninstall.title: Delete the EKS Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the EKS cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

aws eks delete-cluster --name my-cluster

```

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This command helps to remove any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs.

```bash

aws ec2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[*].LoadBalancerArn' --output text | xargs -n 1 aws elbv2 delete-load-balancer --load-balancer-arn

```
commands: aws ec2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[*].LoadBalancerArn' --output text | xargs -n 1 aws elbv2 delete-load-balancer --load-balancer-arn

upgrade.title: Upgrade the EKS Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your cluster, upgrade the Kubernetes version, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > backup.yaml

aws eks update-cluster-version --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup all resources in the cluster.
> Upgrade the cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that the nodes are running the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when kubectl cannot validate the server's certificate.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig is correctly configured with the right cluster context.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in kube-system namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the necessary components are not deployed in the kube-system namespace.

**Fix:**
Check if the EKS cluster was created successfully and components are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient capacity in your availability zones'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This happens when there are not enough resources available in the specified availability zones.

**Fix:**
Try creating the cluster in different availability zones or adjust the instance types.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the EKS cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not running.

**Fix:**
Run 'aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster' to update your kubeconfig.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31 features, including improved scheduling and resource management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain APIs related to legacy networking.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Ensure all existing workloads are compatible with the new API versions before upgrading.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the AWS CLI and kubectl installed and configured.
